mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals rising labor income and consumer spending capacity, which drives aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency in top PAYEMS quintiles does not exceed 60 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A 25 basis point or greater increase in the federal funds rate over a trailing three-month window signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >= 0.25pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between month-over-month changes in industrial production and retail sales captures broad demand momentum; negative co-movement indicates deteriorating macro breadth and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a walk-forward Rank IC above -0.03, or periods with negative co-movement show positive 20-day returns more than 50 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a 0.2 percentage point or greater rise in the unemployment rate over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession risk, triggering equity risk-off and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes in late-cycle expansions produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of cases, or the strategy Sharpe ratio over any 3-year window is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month growth in retail sales signals stronger near-term consumer spending and revenue surprises, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the information is priced in quickly.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or top-quintile RSAFS months fail to deliver positive 5-day returns more than 55 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a sustained manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate earnings visibility and capex planning, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes fail to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances, or the mean forward return during triggers is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between accelerating consumer price inflation and decelerating payroll growth captures stagflationary pressure where rising input costs coincide with weakening labor income; this combination compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term achieves a walk-forward Rank IC above -0.03, or episodes of high CPIAUCSL and low PAYEMS growth show positive 20-day returns more than 50 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects the economy's aggregate output trajectory; positive GDPC1 momentum indicates stronger macro conditions that support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 momentum fails to coincide with positive 60-day returns more than 55 percent of the time.
